user.title: Set up Lightener
user.description: Create a Lightener group, optionally narrow by room, pick the lights, then open **Lightener Studio** to choose the first shape on the graph.

data_description.name: Use a room or area name, e.g. "Living Room"

data_description.area_id: Optional. Use this to shorten the light list on the next screen.

area.title: Filter by area (optional)
area.description: Select a room or area to narrow down the lights on the next screen. With 100+ lights this saves a lot of scrolling. Leave blank to see all lights.

lights.title: Select lights
lights.description: Pick the lights to control. You'll tune each light's brightness curve visually in the **Lightener Studio** in the next step — no number-crunching here.

data_description.brightness: One mapping per line as group:light. Example: "60:100" means at 60% group brightness, "{light_name}" reaches 100%.

error.duplicate: The selection contains the same light more than once.
error.not_a_light: One of the selections is not a light entity.
error.self_reference: A Lightener group cannot control itself.
error.recursive_lightener: A Lightener group cannot control another Lightener group.
error.not_found: One of the selected lights no longer exists.
error.invalid_brightness: Invalid entry "{error_entry}". Use the format "X:Y" where X is 1–100 and Y is 0–100, e.g. "40:100"

create_entry.open_editor: Group created. **[Open Lightener Studio]({editor_url})** to choose a starting shape on the graph.

abort.open_editor: **[Continue in Lightener Studio →]({editor_url})** — pick lights, choose a curve preset, and shape the response on the live graph.

stale_domain_folder_collision.title: Two Lightener Studio folders are on disk
stale_domain_folder_collision.description:
This integration is installed twice: in `custom_components/lightener_studio/` and in `custom_components/lightener/`. Both declare the domain `lightener_studio`. Home Assistant loads only one of them, and which one can change on the next restart.

Cause: HACS cached this repository's old domain (`lightener`) and extracts updates into the old folder.

To fix:
1. Compare the `version` field in each folder's `manifest.json`. If `lightener/` holds the newer version, copy its contents into `lightener_studio/` first. Then delete `custom_components/lightener/`.
2. In HACS, remove the Lightener Studio custom repository and add it again. Do not remove the integration under Settings > Devices & Services; that deletes your groups and curves.
3. Download Lightener Studio in HACS again, then restart Home Assistant.

stale_domain_folder_legacy.title: Old Lightener folder is still on disk
stale_domain_folder_legacy.description:
The folder `custom_components/lightener/` is left over from before this integration was renamed to `lightener_studio`. Home Assistant does not load it, but a future HACS update can extract into it and install a second copy of this integration.

To clean up:
1. Delete the folder `custom_components/lightener/`.
2. In HACS, remove the Lightener Studio custom repository and add it again. Do not remove the integration under Settings > Devices & Services; that deletes your groups and curves.
3. Restart Home Assistant.

init.title: Select lights
init.description: Add or remove lights here. Open **Lightener Studio** in the sidebar to pick presets and tune curves visually — new lights start with a linear curve.

data_description.brightness: One mapping per line as group:light. Example: "60:100" means at 60% group brightness, "{light_name}" reaches 100%.

error.conflict: The group changed while this form was open. Open it again and retry.
error.reload_failed: The group could not be reloaded. Your previous selection was restored.
error.rollback_reload_failed: The update failed and the previous runtime state could not be restored. Open Integrations to retry.
error.not_found: One of the selected lights no longer exists.
error.not_a_light: One of the selections is not a light entity.
error.self_reference: A Lightener group cannot control itself.
error.recursive_lightener: A Lightener group cannot control another Lightener group.
error.duplicate: The selection contains the same light more than once.
error.invalid_brightness: Invalid entry "{error_entry}". Use the format "X:Y" where X is 1–100 and Y is 0–100, e.g. "40:100"
